canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, Sun397 is consistently defined as a dataset for scene recognition or scene classification. A broader set of papers also uses it as a measurement instrument for the more general task of image classification. The dataset is operationalized in different evaluation settings to assess model performance. For instance, one paper uses it to evaluate zero-shot transfer capabilities, grouping it with other "finegrained datasets" for this purpose ("Web-Scale Visual Entity Recognition: An LLM-Driven Data Approach"). In contrast, another study employs it to evaluate few-shot learning performance. The data consistently frames Sun397 as a publicly available resource for evaluating model capabilities in visual tasks.
papers: LoRA-Pro: Are Low-Rank Adapters Properly Optimized?
